content:
I've just enabled lockdep for some nova-core development and got an unusual trace

[ 39.960247] =============================
[ 39.960265] [ BUG: Invalid wait context ]
[ 39.960282] 6.12.0-rc2+ #151 Not tainted
[ 39.960298] -----------------------------
[ 39.960316] modprobe/2006 is trying to lock:
[ 39.960335] ffffa08dd7783a68 (drivers/gpu/nova-core/gsp/sharedq.rs:259){....}-{3:3}, at: _RNvMs0_NtNtCs6v51TV2h8sK_6nova_c3gsp7sharedqNtB5_26GSPSharedQueuesr535_113_018rpc_push+0x34/0x4c0 [nova_core]
[ 39.960413] other info that might help us debug this:
[ 39.960434] context-{4:4}
[ 39.960447] 2 locks held by modprobe/2006:
[ 39.960465] #0: ffffa08dc27581b0 (&dev->mutex){....}-{3:3}, at: __driver_attach+0x111/0x260
[ 39.960505] #1: ffffffffad55ac10 (rcu_read_lock){....}-{1:2}, at: rust_helper_rcu_read_lock+0x11/0x80
[ 39.960545] stack backtrace:
[ 39.960559] CPU: 8 UID: 0 PID: 2006 Comm: modprobe Not tainted 6.12.0-rc2+ #151
[ 39.960586] Hardware name: System manufacturer System Product Name/PRIME X370-PRO, BIOS 6231 08/31/2024
[ 39.960618] Call Trace:
[ 39.960632] <TASK>

I'm not 100% sure how to read this, but I think the problem is a mutex is held, then the rcu read lock is taken and then another mutex is trying to be locked and lock checking says this isn't a good thing, @Simona Vetter do you concur?

Is this a problem with the way the rust sync guards are done? and has anyone else seen this and have any ideas?
actually this seems probably due to some revocable interaction, let me dig a bit more
okay it appears I was taking a bar recovable, and not dropping it, then taking a mutex later, my bad :-)
Dave Airlie has marked this topic as resolved.
yeah, I was thinking this sounds like a missing drop somewhere ...
